phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine recurrent major depression and metabolic syndrome with hip avascular necrosis/femoral neck fracture and acute myeloid leukemia. Additional benign tumors (lipoma, benign renal neoplasm) reflect disparate systems without a unifying mechanism, so this does not represent a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Non-Hodgkin lymphomas with cytopenias
phenotypes.description: Non‑Hodgkin lymphomas and related B‑cell neoplasms (including DLBCL, MALT, mantle cell, and CLL) dominate, accompanied by marrow suppression and cytopenias (neutropenia, pancytopenia, thrombocytopenia), anemia, splenomegaly, and hypogammaglobulinemia. Immune dysfunction is a recurrent feature; some patients also carry Crohn’s disease diagnoses and complications, reflecting comorbid immune‑mediated gastrointestinal disease within this group.

phenotypes.label: Type 1 diabetes with dyslipidemia
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on type 1 diabetes with frequent hyperglycemia and diabetic dyslipidemia. Microalbuminuria is present, indicating early diabetic kidney involvement. Asplenia, qualitative platelet disorders, and opportunistic infections appear in some patients but are secondary to the core endocrine phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Sickle cell/thalassemia with complications
phenotypes.description: Inherited hemoglobinopathies centered on sickle cell disease (HbSS) and thalassemia, including crisis and steady-state presentations, with co-occurring disorders of iron metabolism. Associated cardiomyopathy suggests iron-overload–related cardiac involvement; some records also include acute lymphoid leukemia in remission.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between aortic and valvular disease (aneurysm, aortic dilatation, bicuspid valve) with connective tissue disorder (Ehlers–Danlos), and a separate cluster of allergic/respiratory and dermatologic diagnoses (extrinsic allergic alveolitis, eosinophilic asthma, spongiotic/contact dermatitis), alongside ocular findings (central serous chorioretinopathy, cataract, ischemic optic neuropathy) and other unrelated items. There is no single clinical mechanism tying these together, so this pattern is not a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix gynecologic and other intra‑abdominal malignancies with general abdominal symptoms and postoperative findings without a single unifying clinical focus. This heterogeneity does not define a clear, interpretable condition cluster for clinical use.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are unrelated: diverticulitis of the colon, distal radius/carpal fractures, glossodynia, and subclinical hyperthyroidism/thyrotoxicosis. There is no shared clinical theme across these conditions.

phenotypes.label: Colorectal and anal malignancies
phenotypes.description: Cluster of colorectal and anal cancers, including rectum, rectosigmoid junction, anus, and anal canal. Prominent local anorectal manifestations include hemorrhoids, rectal bleeding, fissures, outlet obstruction, fecal incontinence, and colostomy/ileostomy status, reflecting tumor involvement and local disease effects.

phenotypes.label: Wrist pain and ganglion cyst
phenotypes.description: Localized wrist musculoskeletal disorders featuring wrist arthralgia and stiffness with associated ganglion cysts and tenosynovitis. Presentations include unilateral wrist pain (often left-sided) and reduced range of motion consistent with tendon-sheath inflammation or overuse pathology.

phenotypes.label: Multiple sclerosis and brain neoplasms
phenotypes.description: Central nervous system pathology combining demyelinating disease (multiple sclerosis with optic neuritis, visual disturbance, diplopia) and intracranial neoplasms (primary brain malignancy and meningioma), with related findings such as seizures, headache, hydrocephalus, cerebral edema, and postoperative states. Prominent neuro‑ophthalmic complications include visual field defects, optic atrophy/disc edema, cranial nerve palsies, trigeminal neuralgia, and ocular inflammation (iridocyclitis/keratoconjunctivitis sicca).

phenotypes.label: Bipolar disorder with PTSD comorbidity
phenotypes.description: Patients carry diagnoses of severe mood disorders, dominated by bipolar disorder/bipolar II, with frequent co-occurrence of posttraumatic stress disorder and episodes of major depression with psychotic features. Trauma-related injuries and substance dependence (alcohol/cocaine) appear as comorbidities, consistent with behavioral dysregulation and exposure to accidents or violence.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions center on breast cancer diagnoses, quadrant-specific sites, and postoperative or reconstructive states that are widely coded. This grouping does not define a distinct clinical subgroup and largely reflects broadly shared documentation.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Two distinct patterns are combined: head and neck malignancies with dysphagia, dysphonia, neck mass, nodal involvement, vocal cord disorders, and gastrostomy presence; and separate spinal cord injury sequelae (paraplegia/tetraplegia, neurogenic bladder). These are unrelated clinical themes and do not form a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis with bleeding and complications
phenotypes.description: Ulcerative colitis predominates, with chronic pancolitis/proctitis and rectal hemorrhage leading to iron deficiency anemia. Associated complications appear, including Clostridioides difficile colitis, ileostomy with pouchitis, and primary sclerosing cholangitis.

phenotypes.label: Melanoma and chronic sinonasal disorders
phenotypes.description: Cutaneous malignancies dominate, particularly melanoma and head/neck skin cancers, alongside sinonasal inflammatory and structural disorders such as chronic rhinosinusitis, nasal polyps, septal deviation, and turbinate hypertrophy. Soft‑tissue tumors (including sarcomas and gastrointestinal stromal tumors) are present, and dermatologic/autoimmune conditions like psoriasis, dermatomyositis, and systemic sclerosis cluster in this dermatology/ENT oncology profile.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid cancer and thyroid–parathyroid disorders
phenotypes.description: Thyroid-focused pattern including thyroid carcinoma and nodular thyroid disease (goiter, thyroid nodules), with autoimmune thyroiditis and Graves disease. Prominent hypothyroidism—often postoperative—and thyrotoxicosis co-occur with parathyroid disorders (primary or postsurgical hypoparathyroidism) and mineral abnormalities such as hypocalcemia/hypercalcemia, vitamin D deficiency, and osteoporosis, consistent with post-thyroidectomy sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Onychomycosis and nail dystrophy
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on fungal nail disease (onychomycosis) with nail dystrophy and deformity (onychogryphosis), accompanied by callosity and skin texture changes. Co-occurring peripheral arterial disease and diabetes‑related peripheral angiopathy point to foot involvement in patients with vascular disease or diabetes. Overall, this reflects podiatric dermatologic conditions focused on nails and forefoot skin.

phenotypes.label: Dry eye, cataract, refractive errors
phenotypes.description: Patients exhibit ophthalmic conditions: presbyopia; refractive errors (myopia, hyperopia, astigmatism); cataract; ocular hypertension/glaucoma suspect; and dry eye with blepharitis and meibomian gland dysfunction, often Sjögren’s‑related. Eyelid and periocular skin disease is frequent, including actinic keratosis, basal cell carcinoma, and eyelid/choroidal neoplasms.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions include myeloproliferative and marrow disorders (essential thrombocythemia, myelodysplastic syndromes, monoclonal gammopathies) alongside pancreatic malignancy and extensive musculoskeletal pain/somatic dysfunction diagnoses. Depression in remission is also present. These reflect unrelated clinical domains rather than a single coherent disease pattern.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Common chronic conditions spanning cardiometabolic disease (hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obesity), sleep apnea and GERD, musculoskeletal pain/osteoarthritis, and anxiety/depression, often alongside coronary disease or atrial fibrillation. This reflects patients’ general comorbidity burden rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine syncope and loss of consciousness with ischemic cerebrovascular disease and prothrombotic states, alongside lower‑extremity varicose veins. It also includes unrelated elements such as herpes simplex/vesicular dermatitis, mood and eating disorders, and musculoskeletal injuries. These represent disparate clinical clusters without a single unifying condition.

phenotypes.label: Peripartum complications and ocular injuries
phenotypes.description: Conditions center on pregnancy and peripartum complications such as preeclampsia and postpartum hemorrhage, along with peripartum mood disorders including postpartum depression and manic episodes. It also includes unrelated ocular surface trauma (corneal abrasion, conjunctival injury), so there is no single clinical story tying these together.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Common chronic adult comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms, including hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, obesity, tobacco dependence, mood and anxiety disorders, asthma, and musculoskeletal pain. This reflects a general primary care baseline rather than a single disease process and is broadly present across patients in this cohort.

phenotypes.label: Urothelial carcinoma with hematuria
phenotypes.description: Urothelial (bladder and ureter) malignancy characterized by hematuria and bladder lesions. Co-occurring urologic issues include cystitis/UTIs, dysuria, urinary retention, hydronephrosis from ureteral obstruction, and nephrolithiasis.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading signals combine temporal (giant cell) arteritis—with occasional large‑vessel involvement such as ascending aortic aneurysm—with an unrelated orthopedic problem, medial meniscus derangement. The rest are generic symptoms/findings and do not unify these into a single clinical story.

phenotypes.label: Acute cardiopulmonary and GI symptoms
phenotypes.description: Symptom-dominant cluster centered on chest pain, shortness of breath/dyspnea, syncope, and wheeze, commonly coupled with nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain with bowel changes. Hypertension, diabetes, GERD, and anxiety appear as background comorbidities, indicating an acute cardiopulmonary presentation with accompanying gastrointestinal symptoms rather than a single disease entity.

phenotypes.label: Plantar fasciitis and Achilles tendinopathy
phenotypes.description: Foot and ankle conditions centered on plantar fasciitis, calcaneal spur, and Achilles/tibialis tendinopathy with heel pain and flatfoot. Many also have ankle/foot osteoarthritis, sprain, and stress fractures consistent with overuse and biomechanical strain.

phenotypes.label: Vitreoretinal degeneration and macular disease
phenotypes.description: Ophthalmic pattern dominated by degenerative vitreoretinal and macular disease, including nonexudative and exudative age-related macular degeneration, epiretinal membrane, vitreous degeneration/opacities, lattice degeneration, and chorioretinal scarring. Co-occurring findings include retinal hemorrhage/neovascularization, primary open‑angle glaucoma, cataract (nuclear), tear film insufficiency, and eyelid dermatochalasis—typical of older adults with chronic ocular comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Prostate cancer with BPH and LUTS
phenotypes.description: Male urologic pattern centered on prostate malignancy with elevated PSA and concomitant benign prostatic hyperplasia. Patients exhibit lower urinary tract symptoms (nocturia, urgency, retention, obstruction) and sexual dysfunction (erectile dysfunction, male stress incontinence), with prostatitis and related prostate disorders also represented.

phenotypes.label: Ocular rosacea and eye infections
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on ocular rosacea with concurrent eye infections, reflecting ocular surface inflammation and susceptibility to infectious conjunctival or eyelid disease. Patients likely have chronic eye irritation with episodes of redness, discharge, and infection related to rosacea involvement of the eyelids and ocular surface.

phenotypes.label: Diabetes, hypertension, chronic kidney disease
phenotypes.description: Type 2 diabetes and essential hypertension with hyperlipidemia and obesity, accompanied by chronic kidney disease (stages 3 to 5) and proteinuria. Diabetic complications include retinopathy and peripheral neuropathy; CKD complications include anemia, secondary hyperparathyroidism, renal osteodystrophy, and progression to end-stage renal disease with transplant status and arteriovenous fistula.

phenotypes.label: Mixed: renal cancer and psychiatry
phenotypes.description: This combines renal cell carcinoma and related renal/ureteral conditions (including absent kidney) with neuropsychiatric diagnoses such as ADHD, generalized anxiety, recurrent major depression, and obsessive–compulsive disorder. The inclusion of cutaneous T‑cell lymphoma (mycosis fungoides) further underscores that these are unrelated conditions rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: PTSD, depression, and substance-use remission
phenotypes.description: Chronic post‑traumatic stress disorder with recurrent depression, alongside histories of alcohol and tobacco dependence now in remission. Partial edentulism suggests concurrent poor oral health in this group. Overall this reflects a behavioral health comorbidity profile.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral arterial disease and pituitary tumors
phenotypes.description: This combines distinct themes without a single unifying condition: advanced atherosclerotic disease (peripheral arterial disease, carotid stenosis/occlusion, claudication, retinal vein occlusions) alongside pituitary disorders (adenoma, hypopituitarism, diabetes insipidus). Additional unrelated elements include myasthenia gravis with thymoma and neurofibromatosis, confirming topic merging across disparate systems.

phenotypes.label: Acute cardiorespiratory and GI symptoms
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific acute complaints spanning cardiopulmonary and gastrointestinal systems, including chest pain, dyspnea with wheeze/rales, and nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, and abdominal pain. Also includes general pain, anxiety, bleeding, pruritus, and viral syndrome features, reflecting acute presentations rather than a single disease state.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not coalesce into a single clinical picture. They mix acute kidney injury, electrolyte and acid–base disturbances, sepsis, mood and pain disorders, and scattered hepatic diagnoses, yielding a diffuse, non-actionable pattern rather than a focused phenotype.

phenotypes.label: HIV with HPV-related anogenital neoplasia
phenotypes.description: Clinical pattern centers on HIV infection with frequent sexually transmitted coinfections (HPV, syphilis, genital herpes) and viral hepatitis B/C. There is strong representation of HPV-related anogenital disease—including cervical, vulvar, and anal dysplasia and cancers—and AIDS-defining malignancy such as Kaposi’s sarcoma.

phenotypes.label: Lung cancer with COPD and metastases
phenotypes.description: Primary bronchogenic carcinoma dominates, with upper and lower lobe lung tumors and solitary pulmonary nodules on chest imaging. Respiratory comorbidities and complications include COPD/emphysema, dyspnea, cough, atelectasis, pleural effusion, and pneumothorax, with metastases to intrathoracic nodes, lung, brain, and bone. Post-procedural and postsurgical conditions such as post‑thoracotomy pain, iatrogenic pneumothorax, and absence of a lung are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Actinic damage and skin neoplasms
phenotypes.description: Cutaneous conditions centered on chronic photodamage: actinic keratoses, solar lentigines, seborrheic keratoses, multiple melanocytic nevi (including dysplastic), and related pigmentary changes, with basal and squamous cell carcinomas also represented. This reflects dermatologic comorbidity coding for sun‑related benign and malignant skin lesions and common benign tumors such as hemangiomas and dermatofibromas.

phenotypes.label: Traumatic brain injury with fractures
phenotypes.description: Head trauma with amnesia, attentional deficits, impaired cognition, and cognitive‑communication disorder, often with ataxia, apraxia, and traumatic subdural hemorrhage. Co‑occurring tibial/patellar fractures, nonunion, periprosthetic fractures, and complications or infections of orthopedic implants reflect concurrent orthopedic injuries and hardware‑related problems.
